item.title: Iran said to have only two months of oil storage capacity left as US blockade hits exports
item.description: Prospect of production cuts on the horizon unless solution found to Strait of Hormuz paralysis

item.title: Western-led Kazakh field sets timeline for delayed gas injection start-up
item.description: Karachaganak gears up to extend production plateau with a new facility

item.title: Norwegian swoop for drillship raises questions
item.description: Eldorado Drilling pulled a surprise with its counterbid for the drillship DVD

item.title: Namibia president says 'peace and stability' must not be taken for granted
item.description: 'Local upliftment' and oil revenues critical to create jobs amid employment crisis

item.title: Top Namibian official pinpoints Venus final investment decision date
item.description: TotalEnergies hopes production licence with “good conditions” will help trigger project approval

item.title: Chevron and Rhino firm up two more Orange basin probes
item.description: US major and South African independent confirm exploration and appraisal wells

item.title: $58 billion energy repair bill looms amid Middle East war damage
item.description: Several oil and gas, power and desalination assets have been attacked in the Middle East since the start of the conflict on 28 February

item.title: Asian player eyes acquisitions as it targets 100,000 boepd portfolio
item.description: EOGEPL is India’s largest coalbed methane operator, producing close to 950,000 cubic metres per day of gas in West Bengal

item.title: CNOOC debuts large-scale UAV offshore in China first
item.description: Following successful November test flight, UAV operations now cover 41 offshore platforms and two onshore terminal plants in Beibu Gulf

item.title: Eni steps up exploration offshore Timor-Leste
item.description: Italian player acquiring seismic on asset adjacent to discovered fields

item.title: Saipem wins $400 million contracts with Aramco for giant Safaniya field
item.description: Fabrication activities will be executed locally in Saudi Arabia

item.title: US Department of Energy to study jobs impact of AI use
item.description: DoE aims to collect data that will also show employment impact from data centres and critical minerals supply chain

item.title: Drillship confirmed for Greece's first deepwater well
item.description: Stena Drilling will provide the drillship Stena DrillMAX

item.title: Petrobras invests $450 million in world’s largest seismic monitoring project
item.description: Technology is being installed at Mero pre-salt field offshore Brazil

item.title: BOEM chief looks to make US offshore leasing ‘boring again’
item.description: Acting Director Matt Giacona discusses US Gulf, Alaska and California in exclusive interview with Upstream

item.title: Vaar and Equinor sanction Barents Sea gas project
item.description: Vaar says Goliat Gas Export project will facilitate future gas development through the FPSO

item.title: North Sea operator to cut 100 jobs and restructure business
item.description: Spirit Energy announced plans in 2019 to transition away from oil and gas production

item.title: New operator for stalled gas facility plan at Western-led Kazakh project
item.description: State-run Qazaqgaz will take over Karachaganak gas processing plant from KazMunayGaz, which managed project less than a year

item.title: Oslo-based deepwater driller secures potential multi-year contract
item.description: Potential contract for Borgland Dolphin if finalised could last for years

item.title: Contractor wins $562 million Middle East upstream deal
item.description: Award is part of key package supporting Kuwait’s upstream oil infrastructure development

item.title: Sintana eyes admission to Namibia stock exchange
item.description: Local listing “long overdue” says chief executive

item.title: SBM eyes new Namibia opportunity after TotalEnergies’ Mopane deal
item.description: FPSO supplier keen to capitalise on relationship with French supermajor to be first in line to develop Galp’s Namibia find

item.title: Oil prices retreat on hopes of fresh US-Iran negotiations
item.description: Brent and WTI trading well below $100 per barrel

item.title: ‘Landmark’ Middle East discovery could unlock up to 380 million barrels of resources
item.description: Oman's Masar Petroleum said discovery marks milestone in its growth trajectory

item.title: Aussie E&P takeover still on the cards
item.description: Cue Energy's independent board committee reaffirms its target statement to reject the offer from compatriot Horizon Oil

item.title: COOEC delivers modules for Long Lake expansion project
item.description: Chinese yard says it has built a total of 79 modules for the Canadian oil sands asset

item.title: 'Capitalising on the opportunity': Indie looks to accelerate offshore projects
item.description: Valeura Energy seeking to expedite Thailand developments as oil prices remain robust

item.title: Saudi Aramco poised to award lucrative offshore expansion deals
item.description: Projects are part of Aramco’s long term agreement framework with international contractors, involving brownfield and capacity maintenance projects

item.title: BP to simplify structure with new upstream unit
item.description: BP last overhauled its company structure in 2020

item.title: Petrobras takes final investment decision on second Sergipe-Alagoas FPSO
item.description: P-87 and P-81 FPSOs will enter operation in 2030 and 2031, respectively
